heading: Golden parachute payments
label: 280G Golden parachute payments

Amendments2008—Subsecs. (e), (f). added subsec. (e) and redesignated former subsec. (e) as (f).Pub. L. 110–3431996—Subsec. (b)(6)(D). added subpar. (D).Pub. L. 104–1881988—Subsec. (b)(5)(A). , substituted “section 1361(b) but without regard to paragraph (1)(C) thereof)” for “section 1361(b))” in cl. (i) and inserted at end “Stock described in section 1504(a)(4) shall not be taken into account under clause (ii)(I) if the payment does not adversely affect the shareholder’s redemption and liquidation rights.”Pub. L. 100–647, § 1018(d)(6)Subsec. (b)(5)(B). , inserted at end “The regulations prescribed under subsection (e) shall include regulations providing for the application of this subparagraph in the case of shareholders which are not individuals (including the treatment of nonvoting interests in an entity which is a shareholder) and where an entity holds a de minimis amount of stock in the corporation.”Pub. L. 100–647, § 1018(d)(7)Subsec. (d)(5). , substituted “officer of any member” for “officer or any member”.Pub. L. 100–647, § 1018(d)(8)1986—Subsec. (b)(2)(A). , inserted “For purposes of clause (ii), payments not treated as parachute payments under paragraph (4)(A), (5), or (6) shall not be taken into account.”Pub. L. 99–514, § 1804(j)(6)Subsec. (b)(2)(B). , amended subpar. (B) generally. Prior to amendment, subpar. (B) read as follows: “The term ‘parachute payment’ shall also include any payment in the nature of compensation to (or for the benefit of) a disqualified individual if such payment is pursuant to an agreement which violates any securities laws or regulations.”Pub. L. 99–514, § 1804(j)(7)Subsec. (b)(4). , substituted “Treatment of amounts which taxpayer establishes as reasonable compensation” for “Excess parachute payments reduced to extent taxpayer establishes reasonable compensation” in heading and amended text generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “In the case of any parachute payment described in paragraph (2)(A), the amount of any excess parachute payment shall be reduced by the portion of such payment which the taxpayer establishes by clear and convincing evidence is reasonable compensation for personal services actually rendered. For purposes of the preceding sentence, reasonable compensation shall be first offset against the base amount.”Pub. L. 99–514, § 1804(j)(2)Subsec. (b)(5). , added par. (5).Pub. L. 99–514, § 1804(j)(1)Subsec. (b)(6). , added par. (6).Pub. L. 99–514, § 1804(j)(3)Subsec. (c). , inserted provision defining “highly-compensated individual”.Pub. L. 99–514, § 1804(j)(5)Subsec. (d)(2). , substituted “performed personal services for the corporation” for “was an employee of the corporation”.Pub. L. 99–514, § 1804(j)(8)Subsec. (d)(5). , added par. (5).Pub. L. 99–514, § 1804(j)(4)

children.heading: General rule
children.label: a General rule
children.text: No deduction shall be allowed under this chapter for any excess parachute payment.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The term “excess parachute payment” means an amount equal to the excess of any parachute payment over the portion of the base amount allocated to such payment.

children.label: I
children.text: in the ownership or effective control of the corporation, or

children.label: II
children.text: in the ownership of a substantial portion of the assets of the corporation, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the aggregate present value of the payments in the nature of compensation to (or for the benefit of) such individual which are contingent on such change equals or exceeds an amount equal to 3 times the base amount.

children.heading: Agreements
children.label: B Agreements
children.text: The term “parachute payment” shall also include any payment in the nature of compensation to (or for the benefit of) a disqualified individual if such payment is made pursuant to an agreement which violates any generally enforced securities laws or regulations. In any proceeding involving the issue of whether any payment made to a disqualified individual is a parachute payment on account of a violation of any generally enforced securities laws or regulations, the burden of proof with respect to establishing the occurrence of a violation of such a law or regulation shall be upon the Secretary.

children.heading: Treatment of certain agreements entered into within 1 year before change of ownership
children.label: C Treatment of certain agreements entered into within 1 year before change of ownership
i
children.text: an agreement entered into within 1 year before the change described in subparagraph (A)(i), or

children.label: ii
children.text: an amendment made within such 1-year period of a previous agreement,

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “base amount” means the individual’s annualized includible compensation for the base period.

children.label: i
children.text: the present value of such payment, bears to

children.label: ii
children.text: the aggregate present value of all such payments.

children.heading: Treatment of amounts which taxpayer establishes as reasonable compensation
children.label: 4 Treatment of amounts which taxpayer establishes as reasonable compensation
A
children.text: the amount treated as a parachute payment shall not include the portion of such payment which the taxpayer establishes by clear and convincing evidence is reasonable compensation for personal services to be rendered on or after the date of the change described in paragraph (2)(A)(i), and

children.label: B
children.text: the amount treated as an excess parachute payment shall be reduced by the portion of such payment which the taxpayer establishes by clear and convincing evidence is reasonable compensation for personal services actually rendered before the date of the change described in paragraph (2)(A)(i).

children.heading: Exemption for small business corporations, etc.
children.label: 5 Exemption for small business corporations, etc.
i
children.text: any payment to a disqualified individual with respect to a corporation which (immediately before the change described in paragraph (2)(A)(i)) was a small business corporation (as defined in section 1361(b) but without regard to paragraph (1)(C) thereof), and

children.label: I
children.text: immediately before the change described in paragraph (2)(A)(i), no stock in such corporation was readily tradeable on an established securities market or otherwise, and

children.label: II
children.text: the shareholder approval requirements of subparagraph (B) are met with respect to such payment.

children.label: i
children.text: such payment was approved by a vote of the persons who owned, immediately before the change described in paragraph (2)(A)(i), more than 75 percent of the voting power of all outstanding stock of the corporation, and

children.label: ii
children.text: there was adequate disclosure to shareholders of all material facts concerning all payments which (but for this paragraph) would be parachute payments with respect to a disqualified individual.

children.heading: Exemption for payments under qualified plans
children.label: 6 Exemption for payments under qualified plans
A
children.text: a plan described in section 401(a) which includes a trust exempt from tax under section 501(a),

children.label: B
children.text: an annuity plan described in section 403(a),

children.label: C
children.text: a simplified employee pension (as defined in section 408(k)), or

children.label: D
children.text: a simple retirement account described in section 408(p).
an employee, independent contractor, or other person specified in regulations by the Secretary who performs personal services for any corporation, and
is an officer, shareholder, or highly-compensated individual.

children.heading: Other definitions and special rules
children.label: d Other definitions and special rules

children.heading: Annualized includible compensation for base period
children.label: 1 Annualized includible compensation for base period
A
children.text: was payable by the corporation with respect to which the change in ownership or control described in paragraph (2)(A) of subsection (b) occurs, and

children.label: B
children.text: was includible in the gross income of the disqualified individual for taxable years in the base period.

children.heading: Base period
children.label: 2 Base period
children.text: The term “base period” means the period consisting of the most recent 5 taxable years ending before the date on which the change in ownership or control described in paragraph (2)(A) of subsection (b) occurs (or such portion of such period during which the disqualified individual performed personal services for the corporation).

children.label: A
children.text: shall be treated as a payment, and

children.label: B
children.text: shall be taken into account as its fair market value.

children.heading: Present value
children.label: 4 Present value
children.text: Present value shall be determined by using a discount rate equal to 120 percent of the applicable Federal rate (determined under section 1274(d)), compounded semiannually.

children.heading: Treatment of affiliated groups
children.label: 5 Treatment of affiliated groups
children.text: Except as otherwise provided in regulations, all members of the same affiliated group (as defined in section 1504, determined without regard to section 1504(b)) shall be treated as 1 corporation for purposes of this section. Any person who is an officer of any member of such group shall be treated as an officer of such 1 corporation.

children.heading: Special rule for application to employers participating in the Troubled Assets Relief Program
children.label: e Special rule for application to employers participating in the Troubled Assets Relief Program
A
children.text: Any reference to a disqualified individual (other than in subsection (c)) shall be treated as a reference to a covered executive.

children.label: B
children.text: Any reference to a change described in subsection (b)(2)(A)(i) shall be treated as a reference to an applicable severance from employment of a covered executive, and any reference to a payment contingent on such a change shall be treated as a reference to any payment made during an applicable taxable year of the employer on account of such applicable severance from employment.

children.label: C
children.text: Any reference to a corporation shall be treated as a reference to an applicable employer.

children.label: D
children.text: The provisions of subsections (b)(2)(C), (b)(4), (b)(5), and (d)(5) shall not apply.

children.heading: Definitions
children.label: A Definitions
children.text: Any term used in this subsection which is also used in section 162(m)(5) shall have the meaning given such term by such section.

children.label: i
children.text: by reason of an involuntary termination of the executive by the employer, or

children.label: ii
children.text: in connection with any bankruptcy, liquidation, or receivership of the employer.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: If a payment which is treated as a parachute payment by reason of this subsection is also a parachute payment determined without regard to this subsection, this subsection shall not apply to such payment.

children.label: I
children.text: to carry out the purposes of this subsection and the Emergency Economic Stabilization Act of 2008, including the extent to which this subsection applies in the case of any acquisition, merger, or reorganization of an applicable employer,

children.label: II
children.text: to apply this section and section 4999 in cases where one or more payments with respect to any individual are treated as parachute payments by reason of this subsection, and other payments with respect to such individual are treated as parachute payments under this section without regard to this subsection, and

children.label: III
children.text: to prevent the avoidance of the application of this section through the mischaracterization of a severance from employment as other than an applicable severance from employment.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: f Regulations
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe such regulations as may be necessary or appropriate to carry out the purposes of this section (including regulations for the application of this section in the case of related corporations and in the case of personal service corporations).
